---
title: "Game Predictor"
---

## Project Overview

**wc-sim** is a Python package for simulating international soccer matches using a custom rating system.

The main idea is simple:

- start with current FIFA ranking points
- add a bonus based on historical FIFA World Cup titles
- validate whether this new rating predicts match outcomes better than raw FIFA points
- use the rating to simulate head-to-head matches

## Motivating Question

Can we build a more useful World Cup-style match simulator by combining a team's current FIFA strength with its historical tournament success?

## Project Components

This project includes:

- a Python package for data wrangling, simulation, and validation
- a Streamlit app for head-to-head match simulation
- a tutorial for installation and package use
- a technical report explaining the methodology and results

## Quick Links

- [Tutorial](tutorial.html)
- [Technical Report](report.html)
- [API Reference](reference.html)
- [Streamlit App](https://game-predictor-g66jsocnan5fdee67p8ben.streamlit.app/)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/santiago28b/game-predictor)

## Main Functions

The package currently includes these main functions:

- `calculate_weighted_rating()`
- `compare_rank_changes()`
- `simulate_match()`
- `simulate_match_n()`
- `validate_weighted_rating()`
- `tune_weight()`

## Data Sources

This project uses:

- current FIFA rankings
- a curated table of World Cup title counts
- historical international match results for validation

The data collection and cleaning workflow is documented in the repository, including the notebook used to assemble the dataset.
